title: PHPR08
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was isolated using the RNeasy® Plus Universal Mini Kit (Qiagen, DEU) according to the manual manufacturer’s instruction.,RNAs with poly(A) tails were isolated, and double-stranded cDNA libraries were prepared using TruSeq RNA Kit (Illumina, USA).
data_processing: Tophat2 (v2.1.1; run with parameters '-p 6 -r 50 -b2 -very-sensitive') + Picard MarkDuplicats (v2.2.2) + GATK (v3.5) pipeline was used to detect reliable variants from RNA-seq data. ANNOVAR (v2016-02-01) was used to annotate variants. A/G and C/T mismatches statistified (1) with allele frequency lower than 0.05 in East Asia population or without allele frequency, (2) VQSR marked as 'PASS' were retained as potential RNA editing sites.,Gene counts were calculated by STAR (v2.5.2b + HTSeq htseq-count (v0.9.0) pipeline. Raw counts of two umbilical cord blood were used to remove the influence of blood contamination on gene expressions in placenta.,Editing levels of potential RNA editing sites were defined as the ratio of reads with editing to total reads. If there is no read covering the editing site in a sample, editing ratio for this site in this sample would be assigned as zero.,Transcripts expressed in placenta were assembled from RNA-seq reads by StringTie (v1.3.4) and comapred with known transcripts from Ensembl (GRCh38.84) by Cufflinks cuffcompare (v2.2.1). Then the intron retention events were detected by comparing assembled transcripts with corresponding non-redunant canonical transcripts from GENCODE (v29).,For every intron retention (IR) event, the percent spliced in (PSI) was defined as the ratio of adjusted spliced-in reads and the sum of adjusted spliced-in and spliced-out reads. Adjusted spliced-in reads = number of reads supporting IR events / (IR event length + read length -1). Adjusted spliced-out reads = number of reads skipping corresponding introns / (read length -1).,Genome_build: hg38,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For RNA editing sites: counts and editing ratios at potential RNA editing sites. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: chr, pos, ref, alt, func.refgene, gene.refgene, strand, non-edited.counts, edited.counts, editing.ratio,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For gene expression: raw counts of expressed genes in placenta and umbilical cord blood; counts after removing blood contamination of expressed genes in placenta. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: ENSG id, raw counts (or normalized counts).,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For intron retention (IR) events: counts and PSI values for IR events. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: stringtie transcript id, gene symbol, Chr, IR start, IR end, strand, reads supporting IR, reads not supporing IR, adjusted reads supporting IR, adjusted reads not supporting IR, PSI.

data_processing: Tophat2 (v2.1.1; run with parameters '-p 6 -r 50 -b2 -very-sensitive') + Picard MarkDuplicats (v2.2.2) + GATK (v3.5) pipeline was used to detect reliable variants from RNA-seq data. ANNOVAR (v2016-02-01) was used to annotate variants. A/G and C/T mismatches statistified (1) with allele frequency lower than 0.05 in East Asia population or without allele frequency, (2) VQSR marked as 'PASS' were retained as potential RNA editing sites.,Gene counts were calculated by STAR (v2.5.2b + HTSeq htseq-count (v0.9.0) pipeline. Raw counts of two umbilical cord blood were used to remove the influence of blood contamination on gene expressions in placenta.,Editing levels of potential RNA editing sites were defined as the ratio of reads with editing to total reads. If there is no read covering the editing site in a sample, editing ratio for this site in this sample would be assigned as zero.,Transcripts expressed in placenta were assembled from RNA-seq reads by StringTie (v1.3.4) and comapred with known transcripts from Ensembl (GRCh38.84) by Cufflinks cuffcompare (v2.2.1). Then the intron retention events were detected by comparing assembled transcripts with corresponding non-redunant canonical transcripts from GENCODE (v29).,For every intron retention (IR) event, the percent spliced in (PSI) was defined as the ratio of adjusted spliced-in reads and the sum of adjusted spliced-in and spliced-out reads. Adjusted spliced-in reads = number of reads supporting IR events / (IR event length + read length -1). Adjusted spliced-out reads = number of reads skipping corresponding introns / (read length -1).,Genome_build: hg38,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For RNA editing sites: counts and editing ratios at potential RNA editing sites. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: chr, pos, ref, alt, func.refgene, gene.refgene, strand, non-edited.counts, edited.counts, editing.ratio,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For gene expression: raw counts of expressed genes in placenta and umbilical cord blood; counts after removing blood contamination of expressed genes in placenta. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: ENSG id, raw counts (or normalized counts).,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For intron retention (IR) events: counts and PSI values for IR events. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: stringtie transcript id, gene symbol, Chr, IR start, IR end, strand, reads supporting IR, reads not supporing IR, adjusted reads supporting IR, adjusted reads not supporting IR, PSI.

data_processing: Tophat2 (v2.1.1; run with parameters '-p 6 -r 50 -b2 -very-sensitive') + Picard MarkDuplicats (v2.2.2) + GATK (v3.5) pipeline was used to detect reliable variants from RNA-seq data. ANNOVAR (v2016-02-01) was used to annotate variants. A/G and C/T mismatches statistified (1) with allele frequency lower than 0.05 in East Asia population or without allele frequency, (2) VQSR marked as 'PASS' were retained as potential RNA editing sites.,Gene counts were calculated by STAR (v2.5.2b + HTSeq htseq-count (v0.9.0) pipeline. Raw counts of two umbilical cord blood were used to remove the influence of blood contamination on gene expressions in placenta.,Editing levels of potential RNA editing sites were defined as the ratio of reads with editing to total reads. If there is no read covering the editing site in a sample, editing ratio for this site in this sample would be assigned as zero.,Transcripts expressed in placenta were assembled from RNA-seq reads by StringTie (v1.3.4) and comapred with known transcripts from Ensembl (GRCh38.84) by Cufflinks cuffcompare (v2.2.1). Then the intron retention events were detected by comparing assembled transcripts with corresponding non-redunant canonical transcripts from GENCODE (v29).,For every intron retention (IR) event, the percent spliced in (PSI) was defined as the ratio of adjusted spliced-in reads and the sum of adjusted spliced-in and spliced-out reads. Adjusted spliced-in reads = number of reads supporting IR events / (IR event length + read length -1). Adjusted spliced-out reads = number of reads skipping corresponding introns / (read length -1).,Genome_build: hg38,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For RNA editing sites: counts and editing ratios at potential RNA editing sites. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: chr, pos, ref, alt, func.refgene, gene.refgene, strand, non-edited.counts, edited.counts, editing.ratio,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For gene expression: raw counts of expressed genes in placenta and umbilical cord blood; counts after removing blood contamination of expressed genes in placenta. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: ENSG id, raw counts (or normalized counts).,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For intron retention (IR) events: counts and PSI values for IR events. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: stringtie transcript id, gene symbol, Chr, IR start, IR end, strand, reads supporting IR, reads not supporing IR, adjusted reads supporting IR, adjusted reads not supporting IR, PSI.

data_processing: Tophat2 (v2.1.1; run with parameters '-p 6 -r 50 -b2 -very-sensitive') + Picard MarkDuplicats (v2.2.2) + GATK (v3.5) pipeline was used to detect reliable variants from RNA-seq data. ANNOVAR (v2016-02-01) was used to annotate variants. A/G and C/T mismatches statistified (1) with allele frequency lower than 0.05 in East Asia population or without allele frequency, (2) VQSR marked as 'PASS' were retained as potential RNA editing sites.,Gene counts were calculated by STAR (v2.5.2b + HTSeq htseq-count (v0.9.0) pipeline. Raw counts of two umbilical cord blood were used to remove the influence of blood contamination on gene expressions in placenta.,Editing levels of potential RNA editing sites were defined as the ratio of reads with editing to total reads. If there is no read covering the editing site in a sample, editing ratio for this site in this sample would be assigned as zero.,Transcripts expressed in placenta were assembled from RNA-seq reads by StringTie (v1.3.4) and comapred with known transcripts from Ensembl (GRCh38.84) by Cufflinks cuffcompare (v2.2.1). Then the intron retention events were detected by comparing assembled transcripts with corresponding non-redunant canonical transcripts from GENCODE (v29).,For every intron retention (IR) event, the percent spliced in (PSI) was defined as the ratio of adjusted spliced-in reads and the sum of adjusted spliced-in and spliced-out reads. Adjusted spliced-in reads = number of reads supporting IR events / (IR event length + read length -1). Adjusted spliced-out reads = number of reads skipping corresponding introns / (read length -1).,Genome_build: hg38,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For RNA editing sites: counts and editing ratios at potential RNA editing sites. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: chr, pos, ref, alt, func.refgene, gene.refgene, strand, non-edited.counts, edited.counts, editing.ratio,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For gene expression: raw counts of expressed genes in placenta and umbilical cord blood; counts after removing blood contamination of expressed genes in placenta. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: ENSG id, raw counts (or normalized counts).,Supplementary_files_format_and_content: For intron retention (IR) events: counts and PSI values for IR events. Tab-delimited files are formatted as: stringtie transcript id, gene symbol, Chr, IR start, IR end, strand, reads supporting IR, reads not supporing IR, adjusted reads supporting IR, adjusted reads not supporting IR, PSI.
